blocks.content: Destura let out a screech.
It was likely a vocal organ meant to threaten living beings, but it was useless in space.

blocks.speaker: Son Yujeong
blocks.content: What are these things?
Combined with Nane's thoughts, Destura looked like an inverted human when viewed from above.
Its joints seemed bent backward, making it a chilling sight.

blocks.speaker: Son Yujeong
blocks.content: Surprisingly strong.
But from Son Yujeong's perspective below, it was just a terrifying monster resembling a bug.

blocks.speaker: Son Yujeong
blocks.content: In that case…
Heating her body intensely, Son Yujeong flew through space like a jet, spewing flames.
Nane's eyes sparkled.

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: The Nimbus Cloud.
Seeing the stone monkey's technique, which once disrupted the heavens, in action brought back memories.
Each swing of her staff tore Destura's flesh, but the lack of sound made it feel unreal.
Finally, as Destura disintegrated into mental energy, Son Yujeong lowered her two staffs.

blocks.speaker: Son Yujeong
blocks.content: Phew!
Smoke billowed endlessly from her mouth as if burning logs.
Son Yujeong walked slowly through the cloud obstructing Nane's view.
As she penetrated the barrier of the Sermon, her body, heated like molten metal, quickly cooled.

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: Thank you for dealing with that nuisance. But what brings a restless monkey to the desolate moon?

blocks.speaker: Son Yujeong
blocks.content: You know everything, don't you? I'm not like my grandfather. I'll live as I please.

blocks.speaker: Shura
blocks.content: Son Yujeong, put away your hostility. No matter how reckless you are, such behavior before Buddha is unacceptable.

blocks.speaker: Son Yujeong
blocks.content: Shura.
Son Yujeong frowned.

blocks.speaker: Son Yujeong
blocks.content: You too. Mortasinger was captured. Are you just going to watch as a fellow member of the Council of Ten?

blocks.speaker: Shura
blocks.content: The Council of Ten…
A hint of longing crossed Shura's eyes.

blocks.speaker: Son Yujeong
blocks.content: It disbanded. Since Veron left, there's been no leader to guide us.

blocks.speaker: Shura
blocks.content: And Buddha killed Amita. Yet you stay by his side?
Shura remained silent.

blocks.speaker: Son Yujeong
blocks.content: Veron…

blocks.speaker: Son Yujeong
blocks.content: He had told her to find Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Son Yujeong
blocks.content: I can't do that yet.

blocks.speaker: Son Yujeong
blocks.content: She couldn't follow his last wish because she still didn't know how to 'live'.

blocks.speaker: Son Yujeong
blocks.content: 'In a world filled with lies and emptiness, what does it mean to live?'

blocks.speaker: Son Yujeong
blocks.content: Until she understood the secrets of this world, she wouldn't leave Buddha's side.

blocks.speaker: Son Yujeong
blocks.content: Hmph! What does a thunder monkey know? You're just mad because your friend got captured!
It was true.

blocks.speaker: Son Yujeong
blocks.content: Fine, it has nothing to do with me. But I have business with Buddha. So Shura.
Son Yujeong's eyes glowed gold.
The Golden Eyes of Truth.

blocks.speaker: Son Yujeong
blocks.content: If they could see the truth of all things, Shura's Gestalt would be useless.

blocks.speaker: Son Yujeong
blocks.content: 'Lucky you met your great-grandfather.'
Associating with Mortasinger was far from enlightenment.

blocks.speaker: Son Yujeong
blocks.content: Yet having everything good in the world made Shura envious.

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: So what do you want from me?

blocks.speaker: Son Yujeong
blocks.content: The way to Hell. You must know it. Send me there. I'll get my friend back.

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: Hell…
Of course he knew, but teaching a monkey the enlightenment of a bat would take time.

blocks.speaker: Son Yujeong
blocks.content: Going isn't the problem. It depends on which Hell you want to go to.

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: What do you mean?

blocks.speaker: Son Yujeong
blocks.content: When you bear karma too heavy for reality, Hell will call you. So decide.
A sword emitting frost and another burning with flames appeared on either side of Nane.

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: The Eight Cold Hells or the Eight Hot Hells? The Poisonous Snake Hell or the Mountain of Knives Hell? If not…
Behind Nane, countless swords spread out like a fan, emitting multicolored light.

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: You can name any place you want.
The moon trembled.
It would affect the human planet, but such thoughts were overshadowed by the immense avatar before them.

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: Buddha.
The image of Buddha rising beyond the moon's horizon seemed to be at the edge of the universe.

blocks.speaker: Son Yujeong
blocks.content: Haha! So you're saying you'll send me to Hell. Fine, I accept your challenge!
Shura's eyes narrowed sleepily.

blocks.speaker: Shura
blocks.content: 'That crazy monkey.'
Challenging even after seeing such cosmic grandeur was likely due to an innate mental disorder.

blocks.speaker: Shura
blocks.content: 'But Buddha…'
Son Yujeong's power had drawn out Nane's sincerity and couldn't be underestimated.

blocks.speaker: Son Yujeong
blocks.content: Grrr!
Lowering her stance and twisting her waist, Son Yujeong heated her inorganic body intensely.

blocks.speaker: Son Yujeong
blocks.content: Come.
As Nane's lips curled into a smile, her jet exploded with sparks in her eyes.

blocks.speaker: Nane
blocks.content: Kiaaa!
Her extended staff traced a fiery arc as Sermon surged from the edge of space.
In the Underworld.
After escaping from the Valley of Wails, Ethella and Shagal wandered through hell in search of a village.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: It's too vast.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: Ugh!
While lost in thought, Ethella's arm was scraped by a branch.
Everything in the forest was made of iron, so it was as if she had been slashed by a blade.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: This is getting annoying.
Feeling the same pain through the chain, Shagal strode up to Ethella.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: Aren't you paying attention? If you're going to die, do it alone. Don't drag me into it.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: I'm sorry. But we've been walking for days and still haven't seen anything like a village. We need to think.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: Why should I? The only one shivering here is you. I'm just watching you suffer because you can't achieve what you want.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Enough! We've fought enough! How long are you going to keep harping on me?

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: Forever.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: Do you think I hate you? Far from it. This place is paradise compared to reality. At least you are real. So keep crying. Make it more enjoyable for me.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: You're trash.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Worthless trash. I should have ignored you from the start. There were plenty of others who could deal with trash like you.
In a fit of rage, Shagal pulled Ethella close and forced a kiss.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Don't!
Losing control, Ethella pushed Shagal away and delivered a powerful blow to his jaw.
The chain rattled, and both of them fell to the ground from the impact.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: O
Hit directly by Ethella's counterattack, Shagal struggled to flip over and shrugged his shoulders.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: Hehe! Hehehehe!

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: Ha-haha! I'm going crazy! Ah, really crazy!
Meanwhile, Ethella trembled miserably, holding back her tears.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Why me...

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: It was karma.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Let's endure. We have to overcome this.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Thinking of the pain of Jin Seong Eum, her own troubles seemed slightly comforting.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: We're both too on edge. Let's rest a bit. You don't want to fall prey to demons either, do you?
Trying to manage her emotions superhumanly, Shagal was not in his right mind.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: Hehehe! Aaah, this is driving me mad! Hahahaha!
Angry, Ethella lay down on the ground and turned her back coldly.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: 'I should ignore him.'
Time passed.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Huh?
Realizing she had fallen asleep, Ethella quickly got up.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: What?
She noticed she was no longer in the place where she had fallen asleep, but in a completely different environment with soft soil and a small spring about 10 meters ahead.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: 'Water, water.'
She wouldn't die from not drinking, but dehydration was as torturous here as in reality.
As she crawled toward the water, Shagal sat nearby, holding a clump of dirt to his nose.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: Did you bring me here?
Shagal remained silent for a long time, only after shaking the dirt off his hands did he speak.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: I moved because I smelled water. I was so thirsty I thought I'd go mad.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: 'How could there be water in hell?' The thought was brief, and Ethella, as if bewitched, plunged her lips into the water.
Gulping.
The sound of the water flowing down her throat was refreshingly satisfying to hear.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: Ha! Ha!
As her mind cleared, she finally had the spare capacity to worry about the next thing.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Where are we?

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: I don't know. You know, you were just wandering around in the iron forest, right? It must be because of a magnetic field that disrupts the sense of direction.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Why didn't you say that before...

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Keep talking.
Shagal stood up.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: There must be something to find here. There's water and a smell of corpses. This must be an artificial place created using corpses. Otherwise, there's no way such a cozy spot could exist in hell.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: 'But who could it be? If there's a being in hell that needs water, besides a growth god...' At that moment, the bushes rustled, and Ethella quickly turned her head.
Someone's voice was heard.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: What's this, someone's here?
From the bushes, which resembled animal remains and plants that didn't exist in reality, a group of menacing figures emerged.
Each looked ferocious, fitting for a place called hell.

blocks.speaker: A one-eyed man
blocks.content: A woman? A woman.
She had a purity that didn't fit the dark hell known for harboring corrupt souls.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: What are you?
The seven ruffians didn't even pretend to listen.

blocks.speaker: A man with a forked tongue
blocks.content: These ones have chains. They're burdened with karma. Let's see, what kind of misdeeds...
A man approached from behind without a sound and held an axe to Shagal's neck.

blocks.speaker: A man
blocks.content: Don't move. If your head falls off, the rest of your hell life will be miserably hard.
Two surrounded Shagal while the others approached Ethella.

blocks.speaker: A man
blocks.content: A beauty, huh? Must have mesmerized men in your lifetime. Too precious to be just food.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Stop.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: The moment you touch that woman, you're dead.
Despite feeling chills while speaking, Ethella's pain was his pain.

blocks.speaker: A man
blocks.content: Hahaha! So touching? Fallen into hell but still keeping the love?
The man holding Shagal's back tapped his neck with the axe, threatening him.

blocks.speaker: A man
blocks.content: Don't be mistaken, mate. You're the worst one here. That's why you fell here.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: 'Tap, tap.'
Remembering the rhythm of the taps, Shagal materialized a dagger in his hand.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: Quick-blade.
With incredible speed, he stabbed the one-eyed man's neck, and a sound of slicing the wind followed.
...what?
As the blade stuck and blood spurted, the murderers realized the situation.

blocks.speaker: A man
blocks.content: This bastard!
As the man swung the axe with the same speed, Shagal ducked and stretched out his left hand.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: Ugh!
Grabbing the man's neck while his right hand's quick-blade moved back and forth rapidly.
Pfff-pfff-pfff-pfff!
It seemed to vibrate, and blood and entrails poured from the seventeen wounds in his abdomen.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: Ugh...

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: What, a serial killer?
As the man flipped his eyes and collapsed, Shagal held a new dagger and turned around.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: Who's next?
The forest was silent.
